body:
﻿5004-S12

Protected B when completed

2025

Schedule NB(S12)

New Brunswick Seniors' Home Renovation Tax Credit

Complete this schedule if you have eligible home renovation expenses.

For more information about these credits, go to canada.ca/nb-tax-info.

Attach a copy of this schedule to your paper return. Do not include your
receipts, but keep them in case the Canada Revenue Agency asks to see them
later.

If you need more space, attach an additional sheet of paper.

*** Transcriber's Note: In print, the following table is set up in 5 columns
with the possibility of 15 entries. ***

Date on sales slip or contract ^
Supplier or contractor - Name ^
Supplier or contractor - GST/HST Number (if applicable) ^
Description ^
Amount paid (including all applicable taxes) ^

Line 1: Total amount paid from all sales slips and contracts included in the
table above Total eligible expenses = ^

Line 2: Amount of government assistance received or receivable included in
the amount on line 1 ^

Line 3: Line 1 minus line 2 (maximum $10,000) = ^

Line 4: Amount of eligible expenses included on line 3 being claimed by other
eligible individuals ^

Line 5 and line 60360: Line 3 minus line 4 Home renovation expenses = ^

Line 6: Applicable rate 10%

Line 7: Line 5 multiplied by the percentage from line 6 Enter this amount on
line 47900 of your return. New Brunswick seniors' home renovation tax credit
= ^

Involuntary separation

Line 60890: If, on December 31, 2025, you and your spouse or common-law
partner occupied separate principal residences for medical reasons, you can
both claim the seniors' home renovation tax credit. Enter your spouse's or
common-law partner's address on line 60890. ^

Address of the residence

Do the expenses on line 1 relate to the residence located at the address you
entered on page 1 of your return? If yes, tick this box. ^

If not, enter the address of the residence they relate to: ^

See the privacy notice on your return.
